2605.18632.title: Position: Weight Space Should Be a First-Class Generative AI Modality
2605.18632.paper_summary: Neural network checkpoints have quietly become a large-scale data resource: millions of trained weight vectors now exist, each encoding task-, domain-, and architecture-specific knowledge. This position paper argues that model checkpoints should be treated as a first-class data modality, and that generative modeling in weight space should be standardized as a core machine learning primitive. Recent advances demonstrate that neural weights can be synthesized on demand, often matching fine-tuning performance while reducing adaptation cost by orders of magnitude. We contend that these results reflect an underlying structural fact: high-performing models occupy low-dimensional, highly structured regions of weight space shaped by symmetry, flatness, modularity, and shared subspaces. Building on this view, we organize existing methods into a five-stage pipeline, survey applications where the approach is already practical, and clarify current limits: adapter-scale and conditional generation are advancing rapidly, while unrestricted frontier-scale checkpoint synthesis remains open. Our goal is to shift the community's default mindset from optimizing models per task to sampling models from learned weight distributions, accelerating toward an era in which AI systems routinely improve or create other AI systems.
2605.18632.paper_summary_zh: 神經網絡檢查點已悄然成為一種大規模數據資源：目前存在數百萬個訓練過的權重向量，每個向量都編碼了特定於任務、領域和架構的知識。這篇立場論文主張，模型檢查點應被視為一種一流的數據模式，並且在權重空間中的生成建模應標準化為核心機器學習原語。最近的進展顯示，神經權重可以按需合成，通常在減少適應成本的同時，匹配微調性能。我們認為這些結果反映了一個潛在的結構事實：高性能模型佔據了由對稱性、平坦性、模塊性和共享子空間所塑造的低維、高結構的權重空間區域。在這一觀點的基礎上，我們將現有方法組織成五階段管道，調查該方法已經實用的應用，並澄清當前的限制：適配器規模和條件生成正在迅速發展，而不受限制的前沿規模檢查點合成仍然是開放的。我們的目標是將社群的默認思維模式從針對每個任務優化模型轉變為從學習到的權重分佈中抽樣模型，加速邁向一個AI系統常規地改進或創造其他AI系統的時代。
2605.18632.author: Zhangyang Wang et.al.
2605.18632.authors: Zhangyang Wang, Peihao Wang, Kai Wang
